payload.message: RAG chunking tradeoffs in long-context QA

A good follow-up rule for RAG chunking is to separate retrieval chunks from synthesis chunks. Retrieval chunks should stay compact enough to match a specific question intent, while synthesis can merge adjacent evidence after retrieval. Overlap is helpful only until duplicate passages start dominating top-k results. If latency permits, hierarchical retrieval or sentence-level reranking often outperforms simply increasing chunk size because it keeps recall high without bloating the final context window.
